operations.name: Gate: decide promote vs keep-prose
operations.trigger: A prose prompt crosses a load-bearing threshold: > ~50 lines, OR its output is consumed by code, OR it is reused across > 1 call site
operations.action: Run the promote-trigger checklist (LENGTH / CONSUMED / REUSED, + bonus signals). Zero boxes -> keep prose. One+ box -> promote.
evidence: dspy-sop S1: prompts past ~50 lines are a symptom
dspy-sop S7: do not promote while signature unstable

operations.name: Extract inputs and outputs into named slots
operations.action: Read the prose prompt; list every variable the LM is given (inputs) and every distinct value it must return (outputs). Split fused outputs (e.g. 'category and reason' = two fields).
operations.output: Flat list of typed I/O slots
evidence: dspy-sop S2: field names carry intent; one concept per field

operations.name: Name fields semantically
operations.action: Rename each slot to read like a function parameter (`text`->`ticket`, `out`->`category`). Avoid generic input/output. The name is the optimizer's only pre-data intent signal.
evidence: dspy-sop S2: name like function parameters in well-written code

operations.name: Add descriptions only where the name underspecifies
operations.action: Add InputField(desc=) / OutputField(desc=) ONLY for fields whose name is ambiguous or that need a constraint (format/length/units/allowed values). Leave obvious fields bare.
evidence: dspy-sop S3: cheatsheet leaves `question` bare, adds desc only on `answer`

operations.name: Type closed-set / boolean / numeric outputs
operations.trigger: An output field has a fixed value set, a boolean meaning, or a numeric range
operations.action: Type the OutputField (`Literal[...]`, `bool`, `int`, `float`) instead of `str`, so the type is both documentation and a parse guard, and downstream code stops scraping prose.
evidence: dspy-sop S3: upgrade to class-based Signature when types matter
dspy-sop S5: signature shape is your code's job

operations.name: Defer module choice to [[dspy]]
operations.trigger: Signature shaped; quality may benefit from reasoning
operations.action: Note any module hint (e.g. 'reasoning helps -> likely ChainOfThought') but DO NOT decide here. Hand the shaped Signature to the [[dspy]] skill for module selection / evaluation / compile.
evidence: dspy-sop S4: module-selection table belongs to the dspy library/workflow

operations.name: Re-audit Signature when optimizer plateaus
operations.action: Before blaming the optimizer, loop back: are inputs/outputs truly separated? Do field names carry intent? Ambiguous Signature is the common root cause.
evidence: dspy-sop S8: plateau usually means program/signature ambiguity, not optimizer

operations.name: Pair Signature with a grammar enforcer when machine-validity is mandatory
operations.trigger: Output is consumed by code AND must be syntactically valid (strict JSON/grammar)
operations.action: Keep the Signature for intent shaping + optimization, but add a token-level enforcer (Outlines/Guidance) for the hard syntax guarantee. A typed OutputField pushes toward structure but does not guarantee grammar.
evidence: dspy-sop S9: typed OutputField does not guarantee grammar conformance; use Outlines for that

field_naming_rules: Name like a function parameter, not like a prompt sentence.
Inputs are nouns the LM receives; outputs are nouns it produces; do not mix.
One concept per field; split fused outputs.
Use the most specific type the value can hold (Literal/bool/int over str).
Reserve desc= for what the name cannot say: format, length, units, allowed values, edge cases.

dilemma_cases.title: 80-line mega-prompt: promote whole vs split first
dilemma_cases.decision: Promote, but keep only the I/O shape in code; let instruction/demo prose become optimizer-owned; split fused outputs; type closed-set/boolean outputs.

dilemma_cases.title: One-shot disposable prompt someone wants to 'make robust'
dilemma_cases.decision: CONSUMED justifies a minimal TYPED Signature (parse safety) but NOT optimization. Separate 'promote to typed contract' from 'compile/optimize'.
dilemma_cases.extractable_op: OP-5 yes, refuse compile (S10), boundary S7

anti_patterns: One blob output that should be N typed fields.
Copying whole prose prompt into the docstring (that text is the optimizer's).
Promoting before the I/O contract stabilizes.
Reflexively adding desc= to every field.
Confusing 'promote to Signature' with 'guarantee valid JSON'.
